schema.id: integer, unique, monotonically allocated by scripts/work.sh
schema.title: short descriptive name
schema.type: AFK (no human needed) | HITL (needs human decision)
schema.phase: A | B | C | D | E | F (matches plan.md phases) | X (uncategorized)
schema.parent_id: integer | null — for split-out child issues
schema.discovered_during: integer | null — which issue surfaced this one
schema.blocked_by: integer[] of issue ids that must be done first
schema.acceptance_criteria: array of { text, done } — tickable via `work tick <id>.<n>`
schema.verification: shell command to prove the slice works (cargo test ..., grep, etc.)
schema.north_star_check: behavioral question the agent re-asks at the end of the slice (see docs/NORTH_STAR.md)
schema.notes: free-form notes appended over time via `work note`

issues.title: Create DiffWorkspace module skeleton (private state + intent/effect API)
issues.what_to_build: Add a new `src/app/workspace.rs` (or `src/app/workspace/mod.rs`) exposing `pub struct DiffWorkspace` with all fields PRIVATE, plus `pub fn update(&mut self, intent: DiffWorkspaceIntent, ctx: DiffWorkspaceContext) -> Vec<DiffWorkspaceEffect>` and `pub fn frame(&mut self, ...) -> DiffWorkspaceFrame<'_>`. Both are stubs that compile and round-trip a no-op intent. No behavior moves yet. This issue exists to establish the seam the rest of Phase A will fill in.

acceptance_criteria.text: `DiffWorkspace` struct exists with private fields only (no `pub` on data fields).
`DiffWorkspaceIntent`, `DiffWorkspaceEffect`, `DiffWorkspaceContext`, `DiffWorkspaceFrame<'_>` types exist.
A unit test in `src/app/workspace.rs` constructs a workspace, dispatches a no-op intent, and reads `frame()` round-trip.
`App` holds `workspace: DiffWorkspace` but does not yet route anything through it.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::skeleton && cargo build --profile dev-fast
issues.north_star_check: Did this slice make any new App-side mutation possible? (It should not.) Can a contributor see exactly one door into workspace state? (`update`.)

issues.title: Move Visual-Row Stream into DiffWorkspace with dirty cache
issues.what_to_build: Inside `DiffWorkspace`, hold the cached `rows: Vec<WorkspaceVisualRow>` + `rows_dirty: bool` + `row_height_overrides: HashMap<RowId, u16>`. Implement `frame()` to lazily rebuild only when dirty and return a `&[WorkspaceVisualRow]` slice. Migrate the renderer's row iteration to consume `frame.rows`. No other consumer migrates in this issue.

acceptance_criteria.text: `WorkspaceVisualRow` enum exists with `DiffText`, `InlineReview`, `Spacer`, `FoldSummary` variants (per ADR 0005).
Two consecutive `frame()` calls without intervening mutation perform exactly one rebuild (verified by a build-counter in tests).
Renderer iterates `frame.rows` instead of calling `visual_rows_with_inline_blocks` directly.
Grep shows the renderer no longer calls `visual_rows_with_inline_blocks(...)`.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::rows && rg "visual_rows_with_inline_blocks" src/render src/diff_render | wc -l
issues.north_star_check: Is `frame.rows` the ONLY way the renderer learns about rows? Did the old per-consumer Vec disappear from the renderer path?
issues.notes: [2026-05-28T08:29:05Z] Renderer now reads app-owned workspace frame rows; lazydiff-diffs keeps a temporary DiffWidget fallback for non-app/tests until later row-path deletion slices remove remaining legacy consumers.

issues.title: Migrate scroll consumer to Visual-Row Stream
issues.what_to_build: Delete the scroll-height math in `App` that uses `row_count_for_mode(...)`. Scroll consumes `frame.rows.len()` as the authoritative row total. Scroll mutations become a `Scroll(delta)` intent.

acceptance_criteria.text: All scroll math in `App` reads `frame.rows.len()` (no parallel count).
Scroll mutations go through `DiffWorkspaceIntent::Scroll`.
Grep proof: zero `row_count_for_mode` calls remain on the scroll path in `src/app.rs`.
Test: scrolling past inline rows lands on the correct visual row (no off-by-one).

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::scroll && rg "row_count_for_mode" src/app.rs
issues.north_star_check: Does navigation and scroll now agree on row totals by construction? Did the parallel computation disappear?

issues.title: Migrate keyboard navigation (j/k/cursor) to Visual-Row Stream
issues.what_to_build: Cursor row movement (j, k, gg, G, Ctrl-d/u, motion targets) becomes `DiffWorkspaceIntent::NavigateRow { delta | absolute }`. App no longer mutates cursor row directly. Cursor positions are indices into `frame.rows`; inline rows are landable by default.

acceptance_criteria.text: All keyboard cursor moves dispatch a `NavigateRow*` intent.
Cursor lands on inline review rows when traversing them (no skip).
Grep proof: zero direct `cursor.row =` or `viewer_mut().cursor.*` writes in `src/app.rs`.
Existing j/k/gg/G/Ctrl-d/u tests pass against the new path.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::nav && cargo test -p lazydiff-diffs viewer && rg "viewer_mut\(\)" src/app.rs | wc -l
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor add a new motion (e.g. `]c` next-hunk) as one new `NavigateRow` variant + test, without touching the renderer or App?

issues.title: Migrate mouse cell→row mapping to Visual-Row Stream
issues.what_to_build: Mouse click/move events become a `DiffWorkspaceIntent::MouseAt { cell }` intent. The workspace owner computes `row = scroll.first_visible + click_y` against `frame.rows`. App no longer does mouse → row math; the inline-row click case stops being a special case.

acceptance_criteria.text: Mouse click on an inline review row maps to that inline row (verified by test).
Mouse click on a regular diff row maps to the correct doc row including side filter in split view.
Grep proof: zero ad-hoc mouse-row math in `src/app.rs`.
Side-filtered selection invariant preserved (split-view selection stays on the clicked side).

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::mouse && cargo test -p lazydiff-diffs mouse
issues.north_star_check: Does mouse, keyboard, scroll, and renderer all use the SAME `frame.rows`? Off-by-one in inline rows now impossible?

issues.title: Grep-gate compulsory item: zero legacy row paths outside workspace.rs
issues.what_to_build: Close out the compulsory plan.md item 'True side-by-side visual-row model drives navigation, scrolling, mouse mapping, and renderer iteration.' by confirming the four consumers all read `frame.rows` and the legacy paths are deleted (not deprecated).

acceptance_criteria.text: `rg "row_count_for_mode|visual_rows_with_inline_blocks" src/app.rs src/render src/diff_render` returns zero hits.
`rg "viewer_mut\(\)" src/app.rs` returns zero hits.
plan.md compulsory item is checked off.
A regression test exists asserting that two consecutive frames without state change share a single row-list rebuild.

issues.verification: rg "row_count_for_mode|visual_rows_with_inline_blocks|viewer_mut\(\)" src/app.rs src/render src/diff_render && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace
issues.north_star_check: Did the diff workspace just kill the 'four cooks, one recipe' bug class structurally, not by convention?

issues.title: Inline draft editor as DiffWorkspace modal enum
issues.what_to_build: Replace the scattered `inline_focus: Option<InlineFocus>` + comment editor state on App with a `DiffWorkspace::editor: Option<InlineEditor>` modal enum. Entering/leaving the editor is an intent; cursor inside the editor is internal to the editor enum. App stops touching editor state.

acceptance_criteria.text: `InlineEditor` modal enum (or struct) lives inside `DiffWorkspace`, private.
All editor key handling routes through `DiffWorkspaceIntent`.
Grep proof: zero `inline_focus` references on `App` in `src/app.rs`.
Tests: enter editor, type, submit, cancel — each via intent.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::editor && rg "inline_focus" src/app.rs
issues.north_star_check: Did the editor stop being a special case that App pokes? Is the editor's existence visible only through `update(intent)`?

issues.title: Mouse drag selection as DiffWorkspace modal enum (fixes drag-during-scroll)
issues.what_to_build: Mouse drag becomes a `DragSelection { anchor, current }` modal subflow inside the workspace. Extend-selection-after-scroll works because the same intent receives scroll events.

acceptance_criteria.text: Mouse drag is a single modal subflow inside `DiffWorkspace`.
Drag-while-scrolling extends selection correctly (closes the Section 6 'extend-selection-after-scroll' item).
Side-filtered selection preserved during drag.
Tests cover drag-anchor, drag-extend, drag-during-scroll, drag-release.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::drag
issues.north_star_check: Did 'mouse selection during scroll' stop being a known bug? Is the drag state visible only inside the workspace?

issues.title: Search prompt as DiffWorkspace modal enum
issues.what_to_build: Search prompt (`/`, `n`, `N`) becomes a `SearchPrompt { query, matches, index }` modal subflow inside the workspace. Layout-mode changes invalidate or recompute matches inside the modal, not in App.

acceptance_criteria.text: Search prompt state lives inside `DiffWorkspace`, private.
`/`, `n`, `N` dispatch intents; App no longer mutates search state.
Search column/range math is display-cell/grapheme aware (closes plan.md §4 item).
Tests for exact cursor landing, split left/right, search-during-visual-mode all pass.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::search && cargo test -p lazydiff-diffs search
issues.north_star_check: Is search now testable as a single reducer step? Did search-vs-visual-mode coordination stop being App's job?

issues.title: Text objects: nested + multi-line delimiters, side-filtered
issues.what_to_build: Implement the unfinished text-object work in plan.md §5: `textObjectSearchBounds`, `findDelimitedTextObject`, `findBracketTextObject`, `findQuoteTextObject`, `advanceTextObjectPosition`, `previousTextObjectPosition`, nested delimiters, multi-line objects, newline inclusion flags, side-filtered behavior. HITL because UX of multi-line vs. unsupported cases needs human sign-off before shipping.

acceptance_criteria.text: Nested-delimiter test passes.
Multi-line delimiter test passes.
Newline inclusion flag behavior documented and tested.
Unsupported cases are explicit in UX status messages, not silent.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff-diffs text_objects
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor add a new text-object kind by adding one algorithm + intent variant without touching renderer or App?

issues.title: Interactive validation pass: cursor, selection, search, comments, mouse
issues.what_to_build: Run `target/dev-fast/lazydiff --branch` against real repos and walk through cursor motion, side-filtered selection, search, inline comment lifecycle, draft editor lifecycle, mouse drag, and split-view crossings. Record any regressions as child issues. Closes plan.md §0 'Interactive validation' and §3 'Validate visual behavior interactively'.

acceptance_criteria.text: Validation walkthrough completed against at least two real branches.
Any discovered regression filed as a child issue with reproduction steps.
Acceptance signed off by human owner in this issue's notes.

issues.verification: cargo build --profile dev-fast && LAZYDIFF_KEY_DEBUG=1 LAZYDIFF_THEME=default-dark ./target/dev-fast/lazydiff --branch
issues.north_star_check: Did any of the six bug classes (NORTH_STAR) reappear during the walkthrough?

issues.title: Extract AppShell + AppSurface enum dispatch (router + effect-runner seam)
issues.what_to_build: Per ADR 0006: split today's `App` god struct into `AppShell` (router + effect runner + global-overlay host) and per-surface owners. This first slice only extracts the routing + effect-runner; surface state stays where it is until later issues move it.

acceptance_criteria.text: `AppShell` struct with `active_surface: AppSurface` enum exists.
Input dispatch is a single match on `AppSurface`, no scattered `match` ladders.
Effect runner module exists with one `execute(effect)` entry point.
`App` line count is measurably smaller (record before/after in commit body).

issues.verification: cargo build --profile dev-fast && wc -l src/app.rs
issues.north_star_check: Does the shell still mutate any surface-private field? It should not.

issues.title: Move command palette / file picker / terminal flow under AppShell global-overlay host
issues.what_to_build: Global overlays become a registered set inside `AppShell`. Each overlay has open/close intents; only one is active at a time; the shell decides which surface receives input when an overlay is closed.

acceptance_criteria.text: Overlays are a single registry inside `AppShell`.
Open/close transitions go through intents.
Closing an overlay restores focus to the previously active surface (test).

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff shell::overlays
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor register a new global overlay without editing `AppShell` internals?

issues.title: Centralize tokio::spawn behind EffectRunner; results return as typed intents
issues.what_to_build: Per ADR 0007: all `tokio::spawn` calls move into `EffectRunner`. Results are sent on a single typed channel and dispatched back to the originating surface as intents.

acceptance_criteria.text: Grep proof: zero `tokio::spawn` outside `src/app/effect_runner*`.
Every async result returns as a typed intent variant.
Effect runner has a unit test demonstrating intent round-trip.

issues.verification: rg "tokio::spawn" src | rg -v effect_runner && cargo test -p lazydiff effect_runner
issues.north_star_check: Can a surface's reducer be tested in isolation by feeding it a fake `*Loaded` intent?

issues.title: GenerationToken on PR metadata + PR comments + blob loads
issues.what_to_build: Per ADR 0007: identity-bound async effects carry a `GenerationToken`. On result, the surface checks the token; mismatches drop the result.

acceptance_criteria.text: `GenerationToken` type exists with `surface`, `kind`, `value`.
PR metadata, PR comments, and blob load effects carry tokens.
Unit test: stale `PrCommentsLoaded { token: stale }` does NOT mutate the surface.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::generation_tokens
issues.north_star_check: Did the 'stale forge data overwrites active surface after navigation' bug class become structurally impossible?

issues.title: GenerationToken on review submit + persistence writes
issues.what_to_build: Apply generation tokens to user-initiated submits (review submission, comment post) and persistence writes (notes, drafts). Pending optimistic records only finalize when their token still matches; otherwise reconcile via cache.

acceptance_criteria.text: Submit + persistence effects carry tokens.
Optimistic record finalization is token-checked.
Test: submit then navigate away before result returns — record finalizes in cache, not on surface.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff effect_runner::submit
issues.north_star_check: Did 'optimistic submit lands on wrong record' become impossible?

issues.title: ContributionRegistry skeleton + Command + Keymap entry kinds
issues.what_to_build: Per ADR 0008: a `ContributionRegistry` lives in the app shell. First slice supports `Command` and `Keymap entry` kinds. Both are registered values; commands receive read-only `SurfaceContext` and emit intents.

acceptance_criteria.text: `ContributionRegistry` exists with `register_command` and `register_keymap`.
Commands receive read-only context; no `&mut App` or `&mut Surface`.
Keymap resolution honors specificity (surface > global; user > default).

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff registry
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor register a new command + keybinding in one file without editing `AppShell`?

issues.title: Migrate existing keybindings to registry (built-in defaults only)
issues.what_to_build: Move every existing keybinding from the scattered handlers into registered commands + keymap entries. No new bindings; behavior unchanged.

acceptance_criteria.text: Grep proof: zero hard-coded key matches in surface input paths (all go through the registry).
Existing keybinding tests still pass.
Bindings are listed in code as a single registry file per surface.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff && rg "KeyCode::Char\(" src/app.rs
issues.north_star_check: Adding a new keybinding is now one line in one file; verify by adding a dummy and removing it.

issues.title: Command palette consumes registry instead of hard-coded list
issues.what_to_build: The command palette becomes a consumer of `ContributionRegistry` filtered by the active surface's enablement predicates. Custom palette entries (e.g. dynamic file search) are also a registered kind.

acceptance_criteria.text: Palette content is derived from registry, not hard-coded.
Enablement predicates are respected (test).
Dynamic entry contribution (e.g. file finder results) demonstrated end-to-end.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff command_palette
issues.north_star_check: Adding a new palette entry is now a registry call; the palette UI changed zero lines.

issues.title: Status chrome as Chrome Slot contributions
issues.what_to_build: Per ADR 0008: status-line segments, header chips, footer hints become typed `Chrome Slot` contributions. The renderer composes registered slot values in defined order; no renderer-side conditional product UI.

acceptance_criteria.text: Status segments are registered values, not renderer `if` chains.
Adding a new status chip is one registry call (demonstrated by adding a dummy and removing it).
Slot ordering is explicit and deterministic.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff chrome
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor add a 'branch name' or 'AI risk score' chip without editing the renderer?

issues.title: Inline review rows as InlineRowProducer contributions
issues.what_to_build: Inline review rows (comments, drafts, threads) become `InlineRowProducer` contributions per ADR 0008. The producer is a pure function from `(workspace frame, contribution state) -> Vec<InlineRow>`. Sampled when the Visual-Row Stream rebuilds.

acceptance_criteria.text: Review-row generation moved into a producer; workspace no longer hard-codes review semantics.
A second producer (e.g. test-coverage stub) can be added behind a feature flag and shows correctly.
Visual-row tests pass with both producers registered.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::producers
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor add 'AI risk markers' or 'coverage hints' as a producer without touching the workspace owner?

issues.title: Built-in FoldStrategy contributions (unchanged-context, generated-file, imports)
issues.what_to_build: Per ADR 0005 + ADR 0008: ship the workspace's fold support and three built-in strategies — unchanged-context fold, generated-file fold (matches `*.lock`, `dist/`, snapshots), and imports fold. Toggle is a `ToggleFold(fold_id)` intent; collapsed ranges become `FoldSummary` rows in the Visual-Row Stream.

acceptance_criteria.text: `Fold`, `FoldCandidate`, `FoldStrategy`, `FoldSummary` types exist in workspace.
Three built-in strategies registered and producing candidates on real diffs.
Toggling a fold dirties the cache; the next frame returns the collapsed/expanded stream.
Tests: navigation jumps over collapsed regions correctly; mouse click on `FoldSummary` toggles.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff workspace::folds
issues.north_star_check: Can a contributor add a new fold strategy (e.g. 'fold whitespace-only hunks') as one registered function without touching the workspace owner or renderer?

issues.title: SemanticSurface owner: private scroll/selection/expansion state
issues.what_to_build: Move the semantic screen's interactive state out of `App` into a `SemanticSurface` reducer with private fields and intent/effect API. Second instance of the surface-owner pattern from ADR 0006.

acceptance_criteria.text: `SemanticSurface` exists with private state.
Scroll/selection/expansion go through intents.
Grep proof: zero direct semantic-state mutation on `App` outside the surface module.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff semantic_surface
issues.north_star_check: Does Semantic now use the same shape as Diff Workspace, proving the pattern is reusable?

issues.title: FinderSurface owner
issues.what_to_build: Move finder/file-picker state into `FinderSurface` reducer.

acceptance_criteria.text: `FinderSurface` exists with private state.
Open/query/select go through intents.
Grep proof: zero finder-state mutation on `App` outside the surface module.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff finder_surface
issues.north_star_check: Same shape as Diff Workspace + Semantic? Pattern still holding?

issues.title: ReviewSidebarSurface owner
issues.what_to_build: Move review-sidebar focus/selection/expansion state into `ReviewSidebarSurface` reducer.

acceptance_criteria.text: `ReviewSidebarSurface` exists with private state.
Selection/expansion go through intents.
Cross-surface coordination (sidebar selection → diff jump) is an effect, not a direct mutation.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff review_sidebar_surface
issues.north_star_check: Is cross-surface coordination now an effect (one surface emits, another receives as intent)?

issues.title: CommitListSurface + QueueSurface owners
issues.what_to_build: Move commit-list and home/queue interactive state into surface owners. Forge/auth coupling stays behind the effect runner.

acceptance_criteria.text: `CommitListSurface` and `QueueSurface` exist with private state.
Forge data is consumed from cache via effect-runner intents (no inline IO).
Existing commit-list and queue tests pass.

issues.verification: bash scripts/tui-verify.sh && cargo test -p lazydiff commit_list_surface queue_surface
issues.north_star_check: Did inline forge calls disappear from these surfaces?

issues.title: All persistence + forge writes go through EffectRunner (no inline IO from surfaces)
issues.what_to_build: Audit and migrate every persistence call (notes, drafts, settings) and forge call (submit, post comment) to flow through the effect runner. No surface performs IO inline.

acceptance_criteria.text: Grep proof: zero `fs::write|tokio::fs::write|reqwest|forge_client::*` calls inside surface modules.
Persistence + forge are only invoked from the effect runner.
Errors return as typed `*Failed { token, error }` intents.

issues.verification: rg "fs::write|tokio::fs::write|reqwest|forge_client::" src/app | rg -v effect_runner
issues.north_star_check: Does any surface still perform IO directly? If yes, this slice is not done.
